items.text:
New Package Pickup Procedure — Effective March 3

Beginning Monday, March 3, all student packages will be processed through the newly renovated Package Center on the ground floor of Weller Hall. The former pickup window in the residence lobby will be closed permanently. This change is being made to reduce long lines and to improve the security of delivered items.

Please review the following guidelines before your next delivery:

- Packages may be collected between 8:00 AM and 7:00 PM, Monday through Saturday.
- A valid student ID must be presented at the counter; packages will not be released without one.
- Any package left uncollected for more than fourteen days will be returned to the sender.
- A storage fee of $3.00 per day will be charged for oversized items held longer than one week.

Students will receive an email notification as soon as a package is ready for pickup. Questions about a specific delivery should be directed to the Package Center staff at packages@weller.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must a student do in order to collect a package?

options.A: Pay a storage fee at the counter
options.B: Visit the window in the residence lobby
options.D: Collect the package within seven days

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'A valid student ID must be presented at the counter; packages will not be released without one,' which matches C. A misapplies the storage fee, which is charged only for oversized items held over a week, not required of every student. B points to the old lobby window, which has been closed permanently. D distorts the timeline, since packages are held for fourteen days before being returned, not seven.

questions.stem: The word 'directed' in the last paragraph is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, questions that should be 'directed to the Package Center staff' are meant to be sent or addressed to them, so D is correct. A ('compared') and C ('limited') do not fit the idea of routing a question to someone. B ('delivered') is tempting because the passage discusses packages, but a question is sent rather than physically delivered.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the former pickup window in the residence lobby?

options.A: It will reopen once the renovation is finished.
options.B: It will be used only for oversized packages.
options.C: It will no longer be available to students.
options.D: It will remain open on Sundays.

questions.explanation: The notice says the former lobby window 'will be closed permanently,' so it can be inferred that students can no longer use it, supporting C. A reverses this by suggesting it will reopen. B borrows the passage term 'oversized' but invents a new role for the window. D contradicts the stated hours, which run only Monday through Saturday.
